description_long: This sculpture depicts three human figures, two adult males and a youth, entangled by large serpents. The central figure, muscular and bearded, is dramatically posed with one arm raised and the other gripping a serpent, his face contorted in agony. The two other figures, one to the left and one to the right, are also ensnared by the serpents, their bodies twisted in distress. The serpents coil around the figures' limbs and torsos, adding a dynamic sense of movement and tension. The figures are partially draped with cloth, and the base is textured to resemble stone or earth. The coloration is naturalistic, with flesh tones for the figures, greenish hues for the serpents, and a mossy, weathered look to the base.
description: A dramatic sculpture of three human figures entwined by large green serpents, with naturalistic coloring and a textured, stone-like base.
description_view_0: The front view shows the central bearded figure with an arm raised, flanked by two other figures, all entwined by serpents.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the serpents coiling around the figures and reveals the back of the leftmost figure.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the backs of all three figures, emphasizing the muscular forms and the serpents' winding bodies.
description_view_3: The right side view focuses on the rightmost figure and the dramatic upward reach of the central figure's arm and serpent.
attribute_subject_matter: mythological human figures entwined by serpents
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
